title: narcissus tazetta subsp. dubius
intro: Narcissus tazetta subsp. dubius, also known as the Jonquil or Winter Jonquil, is a type of bulbous perennial plant that belongs to the Amaryllidaceae family, characterized by its small, fragrant white flowers that bloom in winter. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of various respiratory issues, including bronchitis, coughs, and colds, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Narcissus tazetta subsp. dubius include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate congestion, and soothe sore throats, making it a popular natural remedy for respiratory illnesses. The plant's bulbs can be used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to combat colds, flu, and other respiratory infections.
